title: Would you rather — $10B coin flip
html:
Would you rather — $10B coin flip

Source: 2023-01-25 Apple Note. A two-line thought experiment captured mid-junior-year. Yahir's stake on the expected-value vs. survival tension that recurs in his risk-tolerance writing.

TL;DR

  • Coin-flip prompt: "flip a coin — heads = $10 billion, tails = die instantly." Pure St. Petersburg / Kelly-criterion bait.
  • Followed by "Jnosee" — a friend's name, presumably whoever he was discussing the prompt with (single mention, identity unclear; possibly a phonetic capture).
  • Sits inside the same risk-philosophy neighborhood as no runway discipline, disconnected from outcome, and the later mega notebook questions.
  • Notably, he doesn't answer his own question — the value of the note is in the framing, not the resolution.
  • One of the earliest "asymmetric upside" prompts in 2023, predating the Dec 2023 build something turn.

Pattern

This is a discipline-of-asking note, not a decision note. The prompt itself is the artifact — same shape as later piercing prompts he collects in 2025.

Open questions

  • Who is "Jnosee"? Friend, classmate, or phonetic transcription of someone else?
  • What was his actual answer? (Most pre-2023 versions of him probably take the flip.)

Related

  • no runway discipline
  • disconnected from outcome
  • piercing prompts
  • 2024 07 04 questions mega notebook
  • asking the right question
